heading: Review of programs
label: 2414a Review of programs
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to renumbering by .section 2471 of this titlePub. L. 102–573, which directed amendment of section 4208a of the Indian Alcohol and Substance Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act of 1986, was executed to this section, which is section 4208A of the Act, to reflect the probable intent of Congress. See 2010 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 111–211, § 241(a)(4)
Amendments2010—Subsec. (a). inserted “, the Attorney General,” after “the Secretary of the Interior” in introductory provisions. See Codification note above.Pub. L. 111–211

children.text: the various programs established by Federal law providing health services and benefits to Indian tribes, including those relating to mental health and alcohol and substance abuse prevention and treatment,
tribal, State and local, and private health resources and programs,
where facilities to provide such treatment are or should be located, and
the effectiveness of public and private alcohol and substance abuse treatment programs in operation on ,October 27, 1986

children.heading: Dissemination
children.label: b Dissemination
children.text: The results of the review conducted under subsection (a) shall be provided to every Indian tribe as soon as possible for their consideration and use in the development or modification of a Tribal Action Plan.
